name: GoDurham Connect
area: North Durham and East Durham zones
notes: Future goal of 300 passenger trips per month.

name: Morrisville Smart Shuttle
notes: Provided over 89,000 rides from Oct 2021 through 2025.

name: GoWake SmartRide NE
notes: Connects rural areas to larger transit networks.

name: Johnston Quick Ride (JCATS)
notes: Part of the MEE-NC initiative. [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 120,984 total trips across all modes, 29 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: RTP Connect
notes: Collaborative on-demand service.

name: Orange County MOD
notes: On-demand service operating in the county. [NTD 2023: parent agency reported 29,279 total trips across all modes, 21 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: Burke County Micro-Transit
notes: Provided over 5,000 trips in its 2024 trial year.

name: Caldwell County Micro-Transit
notes: Part of the Greenway system which provides 250,000 boardings annually.

name: Wilson RIDE
notes: Monthly rides grew 58% from 9,000 (Mar 2021) to 14,200 (Feb 2022).

name: RideMICRO
area: 5 zones in New Hanover, Pender, and Brunswick counties
notes: 1,730 passengers in June 2024; daily passengers up 800% since 2022 launch. [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 715,814 total trips across all modes, 44 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: GREAT+
notes: Serves an area previously covered by a fixed route.

name: Buzzline
notes: Pilot program supported by NCDOT.

name: GoGastonia
operator: River North Transit, LLC (Tech by Via)
notes: Launched July 2024, replaced all fixed-route buses.

name: JUMP Transit
area: NoDa, Optimist Park, Villa Heights, Howie Acres (Charlotte)
notes: Private service focused on connecting popular neighborhoods.

name: PART On-Demand
area: Zone around Piedmont Triad International Airport (GSO)
notes: Ridership grew from 60,110 in 2023 to 72,661 in 2025.

name: HPTSGo!
notes: Pilot ran through March 2026; no public data released. [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 532,979 total trips across all modes, 18 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: YVEDDI GOTransit
area: Davie, Stokes, Surry, and Yadkin counties
notes: Serves a four-county rural area.

name: Kerr Area Regional Transit (KARTS)
area: Vance, Warren, Franklin, and Granville counties
notes: Part of the MEE-NC initiative to expand on-demand services.

name: Tar River Transit RGP
notes: Rural General Public demand-response service.

name: Arlington Transportation (Via)
fleet: ~90 vehicles · Toyota Sienna hybrid-electric minivans (6-pass); AV fleet of 4 Lexus RX450h + 1 Polaris GEM WAV
notes: Ridership increased tenfold since launch; ~70 vans citywide. First U.S. city to replace fixed-route service entirely with on-demand.

name: Houston Community Connector
area: Second Ward, Third Ward, The Heights, Downtown Houston
notes: Fleet grew from 2 to 19 EVs; 46% of trips connect to METRO transit stops.

name: Tyler Transit MicroTransit
notes: Full conversion from fixed-route service to citywide microtransit in January 2025. [NTD 2024: 220,957 annual trips, 28 peak vehicles]

name: Sugar Land On-Demand
area: Designated zone in Sugar Land (~43,000 residents)
operator: City of Sugar Land (Tech by Via)
notes: 42,934 trips in first year; 4.9/5 average rider rating.

name: DCTA GoZone
operator: Denton County Transportation Authority (Tech by Via)
notes: ~3,000 weekly riders; average 16-minute wait time.

name: GoZone Frisco
notes: Approved under $15M contract through 2029; connects to DART park-and-ride.

name: Trinity Metro On-Demand
area: Multiple zones in Fort Worth/Tarrant County
notes: Multiple zones including Mercantile, North Side, Southeast; integrated with TEXRail commuter rail.

name: Cavazos Connector
area: Fort Cavazos + Killeen, Temple, Belton, Copperas Cove, Harker Heights
operator: Hill Country Transit District / US Army
notes: ~40,000 passengers/month; only microtransit on a continental US Army installation.

name: REAL Microtransit (Rockport/Fulton)
notes: Part of broader rural demand-response network; connects to Corpus Christi.

name: DART GoLink
area: 34 zones across 700 sq mi DART service area: Dallas, Plano, Richardson, Garland, Rowlett, Irving, Farmers Branch, Duncanville, DeSoto, Cedar Hill, Mesquite, and more
operator: Dallas Area Rapid Transit (DART); operated by Transdev since Oct 2024
fare: Standard DART fare (covered by valid DART pass or GoPass card); Uber option available for shorter waits
fleet: Not specified · Mix of DART agency vans (Transdev-operated) and Uber vehicles
notes: North America's largest microtransit operation. Launched 2017 in 4 zones; grew to 34+ zones. 925,000 rides in FY2023. Reduces route subsidy by up to 43% vs. fixed routes. Uber integrated into GoPass app 2024. New Transdev $602M contract (Oct 2024) brought full fleet refresh.

name: Denver Connector
area: Montbello, Gateway, Globeville, Elyria-Swansea, West Denver (expanding)
notes: Expanded from pilot to mature program in 2024; new $6M contract adds Villa Park, Barnum, Westwood, Ruby Hill.

name: Link on Demand (Lone Tree)
operator: City of Lone Tree (Tech by Via)
notes: One of Colorado's earliest microtransit deployments; expanded to Meridian in 2024.

name: Highlands Ranch Microtransit
notes: Launched spring 2025; focused on mobility for older adults.

name: RTD FlexRide
area: 17 zones across Denver metro: Brighton, Broomfield, Broomfield North, DTC, Evergreen, Federal Heights, Golden, Green Mountain, Interlocken/Westmoor, Longmont, Louisville, Parker, Platte Valley, South Jeffco, Superior, Thornton, Wagon Road
booking: RTD FlexRide app, RTD website, Phone
notes: 17 zones consolidated over time; DTC FlexRide merged 8 southeast zones into one in Feb 2026. First/last-mile connector to RTD rail and Park-n-Rides.

name: RIDE Longmont
notes: Launched Dec 9, 2024; over 10,000 rides in first few months; free for first month. Fills RTD coverage gaps and provides first/last-mile connections.

name: DuranGO! Microtransit
fare: $2.00 ($1.00 seniors/disabled); Fort Lewis College students free
notes: Launched Oct 18, 2023; evening and outdoor recreation service. DuranGO! Outside added summer 2025 for rides to trailheads and parks (10am–6pm, June–Sept).

name: Ride Free Lafayette
area: City of Lafayette (with service to Kestrel community in Louisville)
booking: Ride Free Lafayette app, Online, Phone
funding: Local (DRCOG grant, Boulder County Trails & Transit sales tax)
notes: Free, door-to-door service open to all; no trip limits. Funded through successive grant cycles since 2020.

name: Erie Bee
operator: Town of Erie (Tech by Via Mobility Services)
notes: Launched April 21, 2026; free shared rides within Erie service zone. Most trips completed in under 30 minutes.

name: Metro Flex
area: Multiple zones across King County (~100 sq mi)
notes: Nearly 30,000 rides in March 2024, a 20% year-over-year jump; unified three prior microtransit pilots (Via to Transit, Pingo, Community Ride).

name: Zip Shuttle (Community Transit)
area: Alderwood (Lynnwood), Arlington, Darrington, Lake Stevens (Snohomish County)
notes: Surpassed 50,000 boardings in Alderwood zone; three new pilot zones added in late 2024 (Arlington, Darrington, Lake Stevens).

name: Clallam Transit Interlink
area: Sequim (central ~3 sq mi) and Forks (central ~3 sq mi), Clallam County
hours: Same as replaced fixed routes; expanded Sat service in Sequim
notes: Replaced Routes 17 (Forks) and 40 (Sequim); ridership in Forks up 333% in July 2024 after going fare-free; first 7 months of 2024 exceeded last 10 years of ridership.

name: SouthWest Prime
area: Eden Prairie, Chanhassen, Chaska, Victoria, Carver (+ select zones incl. MSP Airport, MOA)
fleet: Not specified · May Mobility autonomous vehicles (late 2024 pilot)
notes: Named 2024 Minnesota Transit System of the Year; launched first autonomous microtransit in the Twin Cities in late 2024 with May Mobility. [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 537,877 total trips across all modes, 42 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: Metro Transit Micro
area: North Minneapolis, Roseville, Woodbury, Bloomington, Blaine
fleet: ~5 vehicles · ADA-accessible smaller transit vans (Spare platform)
notes: Ridership jumped 180% in 2025 to over 120,000 after expanding to Roseville, Woodbury, Bloomington, and Blaine. Switched to Spare platform Nov 2024 with 96% on-time performance.

name: MVTA Connect
area: Apple Valley, Burnsville, Rosemount, Savage (Zone 1); Eagan (Zone 2); Prior Lake, Shakopee (Zone 3)
notes: Fleet of EV minibuses; covers three distinct zones across southern Twin Cities suburbs.

name: Maple Grove My Ride
area: Maple Grove (+ parts of Osseo, Brooklyn Park, Brooklyn Center; connections to Robbinsdale and Plymouth Transit Centers)
booking: My Ride app, Web portal, Phone
notes: On-demand rideshare serving Maple Grove and surrounding suburbs. [NTD 2024: 39,563 annual trips, 10 peak vehicles]

name: Plymouth Click-and-Ride
area: Plymouth (+ Ridgedale, Colonial Square, Golden Valley Center; connections to Maple Grove Transit Station)
notes: On-demand service within Plymouth with connections to Maple Grove Transit Station. [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 212,969 total trips across all modes, 21 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: Ride Gwinnett Microtransit
notes: Expanded to Norcross/Gateway85 CID in September 2024; county aims for 100% transit coverage via microtransit.

name: Via Jersey City
operator: City of Jersey City (Tech by Via)
fleet: ~26+ vehicles · Mix of Mercedes Metris vans and Kia Niro EVs
notes: Surpassed 2 million rides as of Jan 2024; 80% of rides serve people of color; 60% serve low-income residents. Blueprint for city-run microtransit.

name: MicroLink (NJ Transit)
area: Monmouth County and Bergen County zones
notes: Launched April 6, 2026 as a two-year pilot; first-mile/last-mile connector to NJ Transit bus network.

name: CTDOT Microtransit Pilots – SEAT (SE Connecticut)
area: Groton, New London, Stonington, Waterford (Southeastern Connecticut)
notes: Four zones launched under CTDOT statewide pilot: Groton, New London, Stonington HOP, and Waterford. Builds on existing SEAT New London Smart Ride service. [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 1,065,778 total trips across all modes, 29 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: CTDOT Microtransit Pilot – Norwalk Transit
notes: Part of CTDOT statewide pilot; Norwalk Transit also operates pre-existing Wheels 2U Norwalk and Wheels 2U Westport services.

name: gbt connect (Trumbull)
notes: Launched July 2024; connects to GBT fixed routes and Metro-North Railroad.

name: GHTD Link (Enfield/East Windsor)
area: Enfield and portions of East Windsor
notes: Launched April 2024; connects to CTtransit Route 905 at Enfield Square Park & Ride.

name: VTD2GO (Valley Transit District)
area: Ansonia, Derby, and parts of Shelton
notes: Evening-only service launched April 2024; connects to Ansonia Train Station. [NTD 2024: 43,026 annual trips, 12 peak vehicles]

name: Milford Micro
notes: Connects to Metro-North New Haven Line, CT Post Mall, state parks, beaches, and medical facilities. [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 255,594 total trips across all modes, 11 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: StamFORWARD (Stamford)
notes: Launched November 19, 2024; part of CTDOT statewide pilot. Fleet includes 4 electric vehicles.

name: Via NHV (New Haven)
operator: City of New Haven / CTDOT (Tech by Via)
notes: Connecticut's 9th microtransit pilot; part of statewide $19.5M program.

name: River Valley Transit Microtransit
area: Madison, Guilford, and portions of East Hampton and Middletown
notes: Part of CTDOT statewide pilot; River Valley Transit also operates pre-existing XtraMile on-demand service.

name: COTA Plus
notes: 2024 voters approved $8B LinkUS plan including 8 new COTA Plus on-demand zones alongside BRT.

name: Chula Vista Senior Microtransit
operator: City of Chula Vista (Tech by Circuit)
notes: Senior-focused service (riders 55+); serves a fast-growing city with significant senior population growth projected.

name: FlexRide Milwaukee
notes: Celebrated its 50,000th ride in early January 2024; expanded reach with new service provider.

name: Sioux Falls On-Demand
notes: Cited as a national model for integrating on-demand and fixed-route transit; reimagined entire transit network with both modes.

name: MARTA Reach
area: 12 zones: West Atlanta, Kirkwood/Candler Park, County Line, Lakewood, Oakley Industrial, Fulton Industrial, East Point, Hillandale, Candler-McAfee/Belvedere Park, Cedar Grove, Gresham Park, North Fulton
fleet: Not specified · Ford Starcraft ADA-accessible vans (12 zones)
notes: Launched March 7, 2026 as part of MARTA's NextGen Bus Network redesign. Replaces underperforming fixed bus routes in 12 zones; connects to MARTA rail and bus. Fleet of Ford Starcraft ADA-accessible vans.

name: Camden Loop
notes: Surpassed 46,000 rides in first year; 44% of riders use it to commute to work. Connects to Ferry Avenue Station and regional transit.

name: Middlesex County RIDE On Demand
area: New Brunswick, North Brunswick, East Brunswick (Middlesex County)
notes: Launched February 2024 in New Brunswick; expanded to North Brunswick (Nov 2024) and East Brunswick to address food access gaps.

name: Passaic County MOVE
notes: Launched May 20, 2025; fleet of 12-passenger vans. Connects to NJ Transit stations within service area.

name: Via Newark
area: Newark Arts and Education District (city limits)
operator: City of Newark / Invest Newark (Tech by Via)
notes: Launched May 16, 2025 as a 6-month pilot; free weekend-only service connecting residents and visitors to Prudential Center, Rutgers Newark, Newark-Penn Station, and other arts/education destinations.

name: ViaLC (Lorain County)
area: Cities of Lorain and Elyria (Lorain County)
notes: Launched July 2024; neared 150,000 rides by early 2026; reduced cost-per-ride 36% vs fixed-route. $2.7M federal grant to expand hours to reach second/third shift workers.

name: Metro Micro (LA Metro)
area: 8 zones: Watts/Compton, LAX/Inglewood, North Hollywood/Burbank, El Monte, Highland Park/Eagle Rock/Glendale, Altadena/Pasadena/Sierra Madre, Northwest San Fernando Valley, UCLA/Westwood/VA Medical Center
operator: Los Angeles County Metropolitan Transportation Authority (Metro)
notes: One of the largest microtransit deployments in the US; 8 zones across LA County providing first/last-mile connections to Metro rail and bus. [NTD 2024: 20,744 annual trips, 16 peak vehicles]

name: Go West Covina
operator: City of West Covina (Tech by Via)
notes: Launched November 18, 2024; replaced existing Red and Blue shuttle routes Feb 2025. Connects to Metrolink stations in Baldwin Park and Covina. [NTD 2024: 35,978 annual trips, 8 peak vehicles]

name: Connect Torrance
notes: Launched December 2024; expanded service hours and coverage zone in June 2025 after strong ridership growth in first six months.

name: GBM On Demand
area: Green Bay metro area (Alderwood, De Pere, Ashwaubenon, East/West Green Bay zones)
notes: First microtransit service in Wisconsin; relaunched July 2025 consolidating separate daytime zones into one large service area. Paratransit clients may also use the service. [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 889,017 total trips across all modes, 27 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: MetroNow! (Cincinnati Metro)
area: 6+ zones in Hamilton County: Springdale/Sharonville, Northgate/Mt. Healthy, Forest Park/Pleasant Run, Blue Ash/Montgomery (+ Deer Park, Silverton, Kenwood, Madeira), and more
notes: Launched May 2023; surpassed 210,000 trips in first two years. Blue Ash/Montgomery zone added Oct 2025. Serves low-density areas not suited for full-size buses.

name: Tri MyRide (Tri Delta Transit)
area: Antioch/Oakley zone and Pittsburg/Bay Point zone (East Contra Costa County)
operator: Tri Delta Transit (Eastern Contra Costa Transit Authority)
notes: Two service areas near Antioch BART and Pittsburg/Bay Point BART stations. Fleet of 8 dedicated microtransit vehicles. Connects to BART, Amtrak, and fixed routes.

name: OmniRide (Omnitrans)
area: Bloomington, Chino/Chino Hills, and Upland (San Bernardino Valley)
notes: 3 zones across San Bernardino Valley; ridership up 60% YoY for Chino/Chino Hills in FY2025. Replaces underperforming fixed routes; connects warehouse workers to employers.

name: NCTD+ San Marcos
area: San Marcos (10-square-mile zone, North San Diego County)
fleet: Not specified · ADA-accessible vans (up to 8 passengers)
notes: Launched June 2024; fare reduced from $5 to $3 following rider feedback. ~100 weekly riders. Part of NCTD's broader NCTD+ microtransit program.

name: NCTD+ Vista
area: Vista (7-square-mile zone, North San Diego County)
notes: Launched May 19, 2025. Serves 7-square-mile zone including Vista Community Clinic, Brengle Terrace Park, and 3 Sprinter rail stations.

name: El Cajon Microtransit
area: City of El Cajon (San Diego County)
operator: City of El Cajon (Tech by Via)
notes: Launched March 2025; app-based on-demand service within El Cajon service zone.

name: Mid-City GO
area: North Park, City Heights, and Mid-City San Diego communities
operator: North Park Mainstreet / City Heights CDC
notes: Launched February 2025; free electric ride-share connecting Mid-City communities to each other and to the broader transit system.

name: SouthPark Skipper
area: SouthPark municipal service district, Charlotte (~1 sq mile)
funding: Private (SouthPark Community Partners nonprofit, municipal service district funds)
notes: Launched Nov 15, 2024. State's first privately sponsored microtransit; free electric EV fleet. 1-year pilot serving SouthPark commercial core with ~1,000 employers and 32,000 workers.

name: CATS Micro
area: Northern Mecklenburg County (Davidson, Cornelius, Huntersville, north Charlotte)
operator: Charlotte Area Transit System (CATS); operated by MV Transportation
notes: Launched Feb 24, 2025. On-demand shared-ride serving point-to-point within Northern Mecklenburg zone. WAV vehicles available.

name: McDowell Express
operator: McDowell County Transit (NCDOT MEE-NC grant program)
funding: Federal (NCDOT $10.4M MEE-NC grant for 11 rural communities)
notes: Launched May 1, 2025; 1,900+ rides in first 5 months with steady month-over-month growth. Connects rural residents to jobs, healthcare, and education.

name: FAST Connect
area: Cordelia/Green Valley zone and Northeast Fairfield zone (incl. Fairfield-Vacaville Hannigan Train Station, Travis AFB)
operator: City of Fairfield Transit (FAST); Tech by The Routing Company
notes: Launched Sept 5, 2023; replaced 3 fixed routes. 35,000+ passengers; 99% on-time; 4.8/5 rating. Average pickup wait under 15 min. Won METRO 2024 Innovative Solutions Award.

name: Southeastern Connect
area: Mount Hope, Chollas View, Emerald Hills (Southeast San Diego)
operator: SANDAG / Urban Collaborative Project (operated by Via)
funding: Federal, State, Local ($3.8M: SANDAG, City of SD, CA Air Resources Board, CA Energy Commission)
notes: Launched Aug 12, 2025; 4-year pilot. All-EV fleet (max 4 passengers). Serves environmental justice communities with limited transit access.

name: Via West Sacramento
area: City of West Sacramento; expanded Oct 2024 to Downtown Sacramento pilot zones
operator: City of West Sacramento (Tech by Via)
fare: Standard fares apply; discounted for seniors/disabled; free fares for Downtown pilot expired Dec 2024
funding: Local, State (YAQMD Clean Air Funds for pilot expansion)
notes: One of the oldest Via-operated programs in the US. Expanded Oct 7, 2024 to pilot service at Downtown Sacramento locations. Multiple national awards including Smart 50 Award.

name: SV Hopper (Silicon Valley Hopper)
area: Cupertino (citywide) and southern Santa Clara (expanding to Phase 2)
operator: City of Cupertino / City of Santa Clara (Tech by Via)
funding: State ($8.5M CalSTA grant through FY2026-27), Local ($500k VTA grant)
fleet: 13 EVs · incl. 3 WAVs and 9 Kia Niros with bike racks
notes: 13 EVs including 3 WAVs and 9 Kia Niros with bike racks. Santa Clara Phase 1 launched Nov 2023. Cupertino original service continues. Plans to expand to Phase 2 (north Santa Clara).

name: Peery Park Rides
area: Peery Park business district and northwest Sunnyvale (US-101 to Fair Oaks Ave to El Camino Real)
operator: City of Sunnyvale / VTA (operated by Zia/Via)
funding: Local ($1.12M VTA grant + $500k community benefit funds; +$100k city supplemental)
notes: Launched Feb 11, 2025 (official launch Mar 4). 2-year pilot. 3 Chrysler Voyager vans serving 9 destinations in Peery Park district + Caltrain station + downtown Sunnyvale.

name: Go Time! Annapolis
area: City of Annapolis service area (replacing Orange and Purple bus routes)
operator: City of Annapolis Department of Transportation
notes: Launched July 2024; replaced the fixed Orange and Purple bus routes with flexible on-demand service. Pickup within 3/4 mile of original route corridors.

name: SEPTA Go
area: 6 Go Zones in Philadelphia suburbs: 3 in Delaware County (Phase 2, Spring 2027) + 3 more incl. Montgomery County zone (Phase 3, Summer 2027); replacing low-ridership routes incl. 78, 90, 92, 120, 128, 132, 133, 206
notes: Planned launch Spring/Summer 2027 as part of SEPTA's New Bus Network Bus Revolution redesign. 6 on-demand zones replacing underperforming suburban fixed routes. RideCo selected as technology vendor Oct 2023; 48-vehicle fleet planned. First SEPTA microtransit since the short-lived Owl Link pilot (2021–2022).

name: rabbittransit Stop Hopper
area: York, Red Lion/Dallastown, East York, Selinsgrove/Sunbury, Danville-Bloomsburg, Lewisburg-Milton, Gettysburg, Hanover (south-central PA)
notes: Pennsylvania's first microtransit service. Multi-zone coverage across south-central PA. One of 6 PennDOT case-study agencies (2024 report). Seniors ride free with Free Fare ID. Transfer connection from fixed route: $0.40 upcharge.

name: CATAGO!
area: State College / Centre County microtransit zone
notes: Replacement-case microtransit: replaced underperforming fixed route near Penn State. One of 6 PennDOT case-study agencies. Among PA's highest-productivity microtransit zones.

name: CATA GO
notes: Replacement-case microtransit in rural northwest PA. Replaced underperforming fixed routes. One of 6 PennDOT case-study agencies in 2024 statewide study. [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 295,091 total trips across all modes, 46 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: Monroe County Pony Plus (MCTA)
area: Multiple zones in Monroe County (Pocono region)
notes: One of 6 PennDOT case-study agencies in 2024 statewide microtransit report. Serves suburban Pocono area zones; system-wide productivity tracked in PennDOT analysis.

name: MB Freebee (Miami Beach)
area: Mid Beach and portions of North Beach, Miami Beach
notes: 100% electric vehicles. Door-to-door and first/last-mile connectivity to Miami Beach Trolley and Miami-Dade County bus. Part of Freebee's 53-destination Florida network serving ~2M riders/year systemwide.

name: Freebee Kissimmee
area: Downtown Kissimmee and expanded commercial zones (incl. west of John Young Pkwy)
fleet: Not specified · Tesla Model X vehicles
notes: Replaced underperforming Kissimmee Circulator bus. Ridership up 190% 2023–2024; 25,000+ rides in 2024. Serves hospital, City Hall, bus/train stations. Contract renewed through March 2027.

name: Scout (Seminole County)
area: Seminole County (multiple zones replacing most LYNX bus routes)
notes: Launched Oct 2025; replaces most LYNX fixed routes. 35-vehicle fleet across county zones. ~30,000 riders/month by early 2026. Largest single Freebee deployment. LYNX paratransit and core routes retained.

name: MetroConnect SoMi
area: South Miami SoMi district; connections from Coral Gables and Pinecrest
notes: Launched Feb 27, 2025. Replaced former Freebee service. Part of broader MetroConnect network. Connects residents to South Miami's SoMi commercial district.

name: Freebee Downtown Miami & Brickell
area: Downtown Miami, Brickell, Arts & Entertainment District
notes: Launched March 2026. Transitioned from fixed-route circulator to fully on-demand. 9 electric vehicles incl. VW ID vans covering urban core and Brickell.

name: Chandler Flex
area: South Chandler (approx. Chandler Blvd to Chandler Heights Rd, Price Rd to Arizona Ave)
fare: $2.00; $1.00 extra passengers/seniors/WAV riders; students free with ID
funding: State (A for Arizona grant $2M), Federal, Local ($3M expansion grants)
fleet: Not specified · WAV available; Waymo AV integration (overflow)
notes: 100,000+ rides since launch; 3,000+/month. 42% of rides school-related. Integrates Valley Metro bus routes 72, 81, 96, 104, 112, 156, 542. First US city to integrate Waymo autonomous vehicles into public transit network (fall 2025).

name: Bay Transit Express
area: Gloucester County (Gloucester Business District, ~20 sq mi)
funding: Federal (FTA Integrated Mobility Innovation Grant), State (DRPT, ITTF)
notes: 222% ridership increase vs. replaced fixed routes. Expanded from 10 to 20 sq mi after strong demand. Part of DRPT's Virginia Rural Microtransit Deployment Initiative (18-month pilot, now ongoing). [NTD 2022: parent agency reported 119,573 total trips across all modes, 40 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: METGo!
area: City of Norton; Lee, Scott, and Wise counties (Southwest Virginia, ~11 sq mi core)
operator: Mountain Empire Older Citizens (Mountain Empire Transit)
notes: 55,829+ trips since launch. Zero-fare service. Expanded to Big Stone Gap community college and Duffield industrial park. Part of DRPT Virginia Rural Microtransit Deployment Initiative; ridership more than doubled vs. fixed routes.

name: OmniRide Connect
area: Manassas Park; Dumfries, Triangle, Quantico (Prince William County)
notes: Two separate zones: Manassas Park and the Dumfries/Quantico corridor. Dispatched on-demand via app. Complements OmniRide local bus routes.

name: GRTC LINK
area: 6 zones: Azalea Ave/Meadowbridge Rd (Henrico/Hanover), Ashland (Hanover), Broad Rock/Hull Street Rd (Chesterfield), Clover Dale (Chesterfield), Powhatan County, Sandston/Elko (Henrico)
booking: GRTC On the Go app, Phone
funding: Federal, State (DRPT), Local (Henrico, Chesterfield, Hanover, Powhatan)
notes: All 5 planned zones launched by July 2024; Broad Rock added later for 6 total. Wheelchair accessible; ~20-min typical arrival. Serves areas without viable fixed-route coverage.

name: CDTA FLEX+
area: Downtown Albany (Union Station/Amtrak to major downtown destinations)
booking: Ride Pingo app, CDTA Navigator app, Phone
notes: Launched Jan 29, 2024; expanded summer 2024. Connects downtown Albany to Joseph L. Bruno Rail Station (one of busiest Amtrak stations). NY State IMI-funded ($1M allocation for CDTA).

name: TriMet NEXT
area: South Gresham and Powell Valley neighborhoods, Gresham
fleet: Not specified · Up to 6 riders per vehicle; WAV available
notes: Launched publicly June 2025 for seniors and people with disabilities. Connects to 79 bus lines, 5 MAX lines, WES commuter rail. Vehicles hold up to 6 riders. 100% rider satisfaction rating in first month. Earlier paratransit rollout April 2025.

name: SPOT Southwest
area: South Cooper Mountain, River Terrace, Murrayhill, Progress Ridge (Washington County)
notes: Launched January 2026. Door-to-door shared rides; free to all community members. Connects to TriMet frequent bus at Washington Square Transit Center.

name: High Valley Transit Micro
area: Multiple microzones in Summit and Wasatch counties (Park City area); discontinued in Park City municipal limits Apr 2024
fleet: Not specified · WAV available; door-to-door service
notes: 286,887 microtransit trips in 2023 alongside 894,598 fixed-route trips. ~35,000 monthly riders as of early 2025. Integrated with fixed-route buses; WAV and door-to-door service available. Service within Park City municipal limits ended Apr 14, 2024. [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 1,448,366 total trips across all modes, 44 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: Decatur Moves
area: Decatur, Mt. Zion, Forsyth (urban zone); full Macon County (rural zone)
operator: Decatur Public Transit System (DPTS); operated by MV Transportation
funding: State (IDOT Consolidated Vehicle Program, downstate operating assistance), Local
notes: Launched Sept 8, 2025 after delay. 25-van fleet; 3-year $14M MV Transportation contract. Replaced no taxi service in Decatur since COVID. Citywide coverage replacing fixed-route bus gaps.

name: TANK Plus
fare: $5.00 curb-to-curb within zone; $2.50 to fixed-route transfer point
funding: Federal, State, Local (Campbell County Fiscal Court)
notes: Launched Aug 13, 2024. On-demand only (no advance booking). Pickup within 30 minutes. Connects to TANK fixed routes for travel outside zone. Year-long pilot extended after success.

name: MWRTA Catch Connect
area: Wellesley (all addresses); expanded to Hudson and Berlin; connections to Waban/Woodland MBTA stations, Newton Wellesley Hospital, Natick Community Center
notes: Launched Feb 2021 replacing Route 8 fixed bus in Wellesley. Expanded to Hudson/Berlin Feb 2025. Curb-to-curb; 10–30 min average wait. Part of broader MWRTA service area.

name: Go2 (Muskegon Area Transit)
notes: On-demand microtransit sponsored by MATS and operated by Via. Complements 7-route fixed-route network. Connects to Herman Ivory Terminal hub.

name: Richmond Moves
area: City of Richmond (5.6 sq mi citywide zone; 2 BART stations, ferry terminal, Civic Center)
funding: State (CA Clean Mobility Options Voucher Pilot $1M), Local
fleet: 3 vehicles · Plug-in hybrid electric (WAV capable, up to 7 passengers)
notes: Launched April 2022. All-electric plug-in hybrid fleet. Serves predominantly low-income and disadvantaged communities (80% of service area). Connects to BART, Amtrak, AC Transit, and ferry.

name: Calexico On Demand
area: City of Calexico (Imperial County, CA-Mexico border)
funding: State (CA Clean Mobility Options Voucher $1M), Federal
fleet: Not specified · Plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs)
notes: Launched Jan 9, 2023. Plug-in hybrid electric vehicles. Serves seniors, low-income, and youth in a border city historically challenged by poor air quality. Contract extended through 2025 ($3M total). [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 977,774 total trips across all modes, 38 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: MetroConnect (Miami-Dade)
area: 10 zones across Miami-Dade County: Northern Miami-Dade, Northeastern, Westchester, Kendall, Dadeland, South Miami, West Perrine & South Miami Heights, Cutler Bay, Southern Miami-Dade (Civic Center and Waterford zones discontinued Feb 2026)
fare: $3.75/trip; $1.50 discounted when connecting to Metrorail, Metrobus, or South Dade TransitWay
hours: Mon–Fri 6:30am–7:00pm (South Miami: Thu–Sat noon–11pm, Sun noon–8:30pm)
notes: Launched 2020 as GoConnect; rebranded MetroConnect. 10+ zones across Miami-Dade. Feb 2026 restructuring standardized fares ($3.75) and discontinued 2 zones. One of largest public microtransit systems in Florida.

name: ART On Demand (St. Lucie County)
area: 3 zones: SW Port St. Lucie (Zone 1), St. Lucie West/Torino (Zone 2), SW Fort Pierce/Treasure Coast Airport (Zone 3)
operator: St. Lucie County Area Regional Transit (ART)
booking: Rides on Demand app (rebranded Sept 2025), Phone
notes: Won 2024 NACo Achievement Award (Best in Category, Transportation). 6,000+ trips/month. App upgraded to 'Rides on Demand' Sept 2, 2025. Inter-zone travel uses designated meet-up points.

name: Freebee Miami Gardens
notes: Launched November 2025. Fleet growing from 6 to 10 vehicles by early 2026 due to strong demand. Part of Freebee's 53-destination Florida network.

name: Freebee Palmetto Bay
notes: 6,400+ rides in 2025. Fleet doubled to 4 vehicles Feb 2026. First/last-mile connectivity to South Dade TransitWay BRT corridor.

name: Freebee Wynwood & Midtown
area: Wynwood Arts District, Midtown Miami, Edgewater; connection to Metromover at 15th St station
notes: Operated by Wynwood BID. Connects Arts District to Edgewater, Midtown, Downtown/Brickell via Metromover. Fleet expanded to 4 VW ID vans Feb 2026.

name: The HOP Microtransit (Hill Country Transit District)
area: 9 microtransit zones: Belton, Cameron, Copperas Cove, Gatesville, Harker Heights, Killeen, Lampasas, Salado, Temple (Bell, Coryell, Milam counties)
booking: The HOP Rider App, Online, Phone (254-933-3700)
notes: Launched Sept 2024. Replaced multiple fixed routes with 36 microtransit vehicles across 9 zones. 46,000 boardings in Jan 2025 (highest month to date). Also offers Urban Regional Commuter (fixed schedule, Copperas Cove–Temple) and Rural Demand Response.

name: Pierce Transit Runner
area: 5 zones in Pierce County: Spanaway North, Spanaway South, Puyallup, Tideflats, Ruston; plus Gig Harbor along Rt 16 corridor
booking: Pierce Transit Runner app, Phone (253-581-8000 opt. 4)
fare: Same as Pierce Transit bus ($2.00 adult, $1.00 reduced); Youth 18 and under free
notes: 5+ zones across Pierce County. Via-powered. ORCA card, day pass, and app payment accepted. Zones adjusted June 2025 based on ridership data.

name: Metro Link (Kalamazoo Metro)
area: Peripheral zones of Kalamazoo metro area (Comstock, eastern/western suburbs connecting to KMetro bus routes)
operator: Kalamazoo Metro / Central County Transportation Authority (Via)
fare: $1.50 (same as bus); senior discounts available; includes one bus transfer
notes: Launched April 15, 2024. Pilot through 2025; included in 2025 millage renewal. Extends reach of KMetro's 21-route bus network into lower-density areas. Via-powered corner-to-corner service.

name: MicroCAT (Charlottesville Area Transit)
area: Pantops area and Route 29 North corridor, Albemarle County
notes: Launched Oct 30, 2023. Extended beyond initial one-year pilot into 2025. Hybrid electric vehicles. Connects Pantops and Rt 29 N to Charlottesville/UVA fixed-route buses. 'Everything on the table' for expansion per CAT Director.

name: Via Mobility - Weld County
notes: Serves unincorporated communities; advance booking strongly encouraged.

name: Mountain Village Dial-A-Ride
fleet: Shuttles and SUVs (operated by Telluride Express)
notes: App-based microtransit for the resort community linking to the gondola.

name: Downtowner Aspen
notes: Operates an electric fleet in the downtown Aspen core.

name: Transfort Dial-A-Ride
notes: Primarily ADA paratransit complying with fixed-route span.

name: COLT Paratransit
notes: Paratransit complementing local fixed routes.

name: GET Call-N-Ride
notes: ADA and older adult demand response.

name: BATS
notes: General public dial-a-ride within Berthoud.

name: All Points Transit DAR
notes: Pre-scheduled door-to-door, open to the general public. Same-day only for medical.

name: Prowers Area Transit (PATS)
notes: General public demand response.

name: La Junta Transit
notes: Curb-to-curb service for the general public.

name: Bent County Transit
notes: Public dial-a-ride.

name: Steamboat Springs DAR
notes: Complementary paratransit to the fare-free fixed route.

name: Core Transit Paratransit
notes: Replaced ECO Transit in 2024; ADA specific paratransit.

name: Lakewood Rides
notes: Older adults (60+) and disabled residents.

name: Larimer Lift

name: Kiowa County Transit
notes: General public demand-response.

name: Metro Mobility

notes: ADA paratransit for the Grand Valley.

name: Opportunity Bus

name: Via Paratransit & DAR
notes: Door-to-door transit for older adults and disabled residents.

notes: App-based microtransit shuttle replacing certain local routes. [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 541,155 total trips across all modes, 8 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: Snowmass Dial-a-Ride
notes: Provides door-to-door service within Snowmass Village.

name: County Express

name: RFTA Paratransit
notes: Paratransit service for ADA eligible riders.

name: Broomfield Easy Ride
notes: Provides transit for seniors and residents with disabilities.

name: Fremont County Transit
notes: General public demand-response.

name: Summit Stage Paratransit

name: Town of Vail Paratransit
notes: Complementary ADA paratransit for the resort town fixed routes.

name: The Lift Paratransit
notes: ADA paratransit and night-time demand response in Winter Park.

name: SMART Paratransit
notes: Provides ADA Paratransit alongside fixed regional routes.

name: Envida Transit

name: Fountain Transit DAR
notes: Dial-A-Ride complementing the city's fixed route.

name: SCCOG Transit

name: Gunnison Valley RTA Paratransit

name: Mountain Express DAR

name: SRC Transit

name: SacRT Flex

name: GoMicro
notes: Replaced low-performing fixed routes.

name: Ride Plus
notes: Geared towards equity priority communities.

name: Pa'Lante
notes: All-electric, community-focused fleet.

name: SunRide
notes: Connects neighborhoods to major transit hubs.

name: TART Connect
notes: High seasonal variation in ridership. [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 902,693 total trips across all modes, 47 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: OC Flex
notes: Unlimited rides with a valid metrolink pass.

name: Wheels Dial-A-Ride
notes: General public paratransit expansion.

name: ParaCruz / On-Demand
notes: Shared ride paratransit.

name: MST On Call
notes: Serves deep rural communities like South County.

name: SLO Transit Microtransit
notes: Launched to reduce fixed route strain. [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 575,946 total trips across all modes, 18 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

name: GO Now
notes: Serves South Oxnard and Port Hueneme.

name: Van Go!

notes: Merger of former Modesto Area Express programs.

name: Handy Ride

name: EVE (Everyone Ventures Everywhere)
notes: Replacing legacy FRAN service with a new ZEV fleet.
